title: Removals in Sussex: A Local Guide | Wolves Removals
meta: An honest, practical guide to professional removals across Sussex — planning, costs, timings and choosing the right local mover.
h1: Removals in Sussex: What a Smooth Local Move Really Takes
lead: Moving home anywhere in Sussex brings its own quirks — narrow lanes in the villages, parking restrictions in the seaside towns, and the rolling hills that make a removal lorry work for its living. This guide walks you through how professional removals in Sussex actually work, so you can plan with confidence rather than crossed fingers.
body:
Sussex is one of the most rewarding parts of the country to live in, and one of the more interesting places to move within. From the chalk-cut paths of the South Downs to the Regency terraces of Brighton, and from the market squares of Horsham and Chichester to the quiet weald villages around Pulborough, no two moves here look quite the same. As a family-run firm based near Pulborough and trading since 2016, we have learned that good removals in Sussex are less about brute force and more about local knowledge, sensible planning and honest communication. This guide pulls together what we wish every customer knew before moving day.

Why Sussex moves are a little different

If you have only ever moved within a city of wide, straight roads, Sussex can catch you out. The county is a patchwork of distinct landscapes, and each presents its own practical challenges for a removal team.

Inland, the Weald is dotted with period cottages, listed buildings and homes reached by single-track lanes with passing places. A full-size removal lorry simply cannot always get to the door, so a good firm will check access in advance and bring a smaller shuttle vehicle if needed. On the coast — think Worthing, Brighton and Hove — the issue is rarely the roads and almost always the parking. Resident permit zones, loading bays and double-yellow lines can turn a straightforward carry into an obstacle course unless someone has arranged a parking suspension or dispensation with the council beforehand.

Then there is the terrain itself. The South Downs are beautiful, but steep driveways, flights of garden steps and split-level homes all add time and care to a move. None of this is a problem when it is anticipated. It only becomes a problem when nobody has thought about it until the lorry is idling outside. This is exactly why we encourage a proper survey rather than a guess over the phone for anything larger than a one-bed flat.

The services that make up a Sussex move

“Removals” is really an umbrella term for several services that you can mix and match to suit your budget and your nerves. Understanding what each one does helps you avoid paying for things you do not need — and avoid skimping on the things you really do.

Full house removals

This is the classic end-to-end service: a trained crew arrives, loads everything safely, transports it and unloads it in the right rooms at the other end. Our house removals service is built around protecting your belongings in transit, from wrapping upholstery to securing wardrobes and white goods. For most family homes this is the backbone of the move, and everything else is added around it.

Man and van

For smaller jobs — a studio flat, a few large items, a partial move or a single delivery — a full crew is overkill. Our man and van service starts from £80 and gives you a driver and vehicle without the cost of a large team and lorry. It is ideal for students, downsizers and anyone moving locally within a town. If you are weighing this up, our pricing page sets out how the options compare.

Packing, fragile items and specialist goods

Packing is where most people underestimate the time involved. A three-bedroom house can take a fortnight of evenings to pack properly. If that fills you with dread, our full packing service takes the whole job off your hands, and our fragile packing service handles glassware, mirrors, ceramics and art with the right materials and technique. Sussex has more than its share of period homes filled with treasured pieces, so we also offer dedicated antiques moving and piano moving for the items that genuinely cannot be replaced. As a LAPADA member, we take the handling of fine and antique pieces seriously.

Storage

Moves rarely line up neatly. Completion dates slip, chains stall, and sometimes you simply need to clear a property before the new one is ready. Our storage solutions bridge that gap, whether you need short-term storage for a few weeks between homes or long-term storage while you travel, renovate or decide what to keep. If you are not sure how much space you need, the storage calculator gives you a quick estimate.

How much do removals in Sussex cost?

Honest answer: it depends, and anyone who quotes a flat figure sight-unseen is guessing. The real drivers of cost are the volume of your belongings, the access at both properties, the distance travelled, and how much packing and dismantling you want the crew to do.

  • Volume — the cubic footage of your possessions sets the size of vehicle and crew. Decluttering before you move is the single most effective way to reduce this.
  • Access — long carries, stairs, no parking near the door and awkward lanes all add labour and time.
  • Distance — a cross-town move in Worthing is very different from a relocation from Chichester to Kent or further afield.
  • Add-ons — packing, dismantling and reassembling furniture, and storage all sit on top of the core transport cost.

Rather than chase the cheapest headline number, look for a transparent quote that lists what is and is not included. A man and van job starting from £80 and a fully packed four-bedroom relocation are not the same service, and they should not be priced as if they were. You can request a quote from us and we will be straight with you about what your particular move involves.

Where we work across Sussex and beyond

We cover the whole of West Sussex and East Sussex, along with neighbouring Surrey, Hampshire and Kent. We also handle nationwide moves and, for those relocating abroad, full international removals across the EU.

Locally, we are regularly on the road in the county's main towns. We handle removals in Worthing and along the coast into Brighton and Hove, inland to Horsham and the cathedral city of Chichester, and through the mid-Sussex towns of Haywards Heath and Burgess Hill. You can see the full list on our locations page. Knowing these places well — their roads, their parking, their housing stock — is what lets us plan a move that runs on time.

A realistic timeline for your move

The smoothest Sussex moves are the ones that start early. Here is roughly how the run-up tends to look.

Six to eight weeks out

Get quotes and book your removal firm, especially if your move falls in summer or at the end of a month when demand peaks. This is also the time to begin decluttering in earnest — every box you do not move is money saved and effort spared.

Three to four weeks out

If you are packing yourself, order your packing materials and start with rooms you rarely use: the loft, the spare room, out-of-season clothing and books. Begin notifying utilities, councils and the usual list of address changes.

One to two weeks out

Confirm logistics with your removal team, arrange any parking suspensions with the council, and pack everything except daily essentials. Run down the freezer and use up perishables.

The final days

Pack an essentials box — kettle, mugs, chargers, medication, a change of clothes and basic tools — and keep it with you rather than in the lorry. Defrost the freezer the night before. For a fuller checklist, our guide to helpful moving tips walks through the details.

How to choose a removal company in Sussex

The removals industry is unregulated, which means quality varies enormously. A few sensible checks separate the reliable firms from the ones you will regret.

  • Insurance — ask what cover is in place. We are fully insured with liability cover up to £10 million, so your belongings are protected in transit.
  • Verified reviews — look for an independent, verified record rather than a handful of reviews on the firm's own website. We are Checkatrade-verified, which means our reviews come from real, confirmed customers.
  • Specialist credentials — if you own antiques or fine art, our LAPADA membership reflects a genuine standard of care for valuable pieces.
  • A proper quote — a firm that wants to survey the job, ask about access and explain its pricing is a firm that takes the work seriously.
  • Local presence — a Sussex-based team that knows the lanes, the parking and the towns will simply move you more efficiently than an out-of-area firm learning as it goes.

Be wary of unusually low quotes given without any questions, of cash-only operators with no traceable insurance, and of anyone unwilling to put the agreed scope in writing. The cheapest move can become the most expensive one if something is damaged and there is no cover behind it.

Common Sussex moving headaches — and how to avoid them

Over the years a handful of issues come up again and again. Forewarned is forearmed.

Parking in the coastal towns

In Worthing, Brighton and Hove, kerbside space is precious. Contact the council a couple of weeks ahead about a parking bay suspension on moving day. It costs a little but saves a long, exhausting carry from a distant space.

Listed and period properties

Sussex is rich in period homes with narrow doorways, tight staircases and original features you will not want scratched. Measure your largest items against the access at the new property before the day. Sometimes a sofa has to come out through a window — better to know that in advance than discover it at 4pm.

Rural access

If your new home is down a farm track or up a steep, unmade drive, tell your removal firm. A large lorry may need to park on the lane while smaller loads are shuttled to the door, which is straightforward when planned and chaotic when it is a surprise.

The chain that will not settle

Property chains slip. If your completion date moves, short-term storage and a flexible crew keep your possessions safe and your stress low rather than forcing a risky same-day double move.

Moving a business, not just a home

Not every Sussex move is a house move. The county has a thriving base of small businesses, professional offices and independent shops, and relocating one carries its own pressures — chiefly the need to minimise downtime. A business that cannot trade is a business losing money, so the goal of any commercial move is to be back up and running as fast as possible. Our commercial removals service is built around exactly that: careful planning of the sequence in which areas are packed and unpacked, weekend or out-of-hours moves where they reduce disruption, and methodical labelling so that IT, files and furniture land in the right place ready to use. The same principles apply whether you are shifting a two-person studio in Brighton or an office floor in Chichester.

Sometimes a move is also an opportunity to clear out years of accumulation — old stock, redundant furniture, the contents of a garage or loft that have not seen daylight in a decade. Our house clearance service handles the responsible disposal of everything you no longer need, so you arrive at the new place with only what you actually want. It is a particularly useful service when clearing a property after a bereavement or when downsizing, taking a difficult job off your hands at a difficult time.

Making the most of your new Sussex home

Once the boxes are in, resist the urge to unpack everything at once. Set up the kitchen and bedrooms first so you can eat and sleep properly, then work through the rest at a sensible pace. Take meter readings on day one, locate the stopcock and fuse box, and introduce yourself to the neighbours — in Sussex's smaller towns and villages, a friendly word goes a long way.

Whether you are moving across town or across the county, a well-planned removal is one of the most satisfying fresh starts there is. If you would like a clear, no-pressure quote for your move anywhere in Sussex, give us a call on 01903 893731 or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk — we are always happy to talk through the details before you commit.
faqs: How far in advance should I book removals in Sussex?

Aim to book six to eight weeks ahead, and earlier still if your move falls in summer or at the end of a month, when demand for removal teams across Sussex is at its highest. Booking early also gives you the pick of dates and time to declutter properly.
Do you cover the whole of Sussex?

Yes. We cover all of West Sussex and East Sussex, including Worthing, Brighton and Hove, Horsham, Chichester, Haywards Heath and Burgess Hill, plus neighbouring Surrey, Hampshire and Kent. We also handle nationwide and EU moves.
What does a Sussex removal actually cost?

It depends on the volume of your belongings, access at both properties, distance and the amount of packing involved. A man and van job starts from £80, while a fully packed family move is naturally more. The best approach is a transparent, itemised quote rather than a flat guess.
Are you insured if something gets damaged?

Yes. We are fully insured with liability cover up to £10 million, so your possessions are protected in transit. We are also Checkatrade-verified and a LAPADA member for the careful handling of antiques and fine items.
Can you help if my completion date moves?

Absolutely. Slipping completion dates are common in property chains. We offer short-term storage to bridge the gap, so your belongings stay safe and you avoid the stress of a risky same-day double move.
